mission.title: tikv: Improve robustness of Backup/Restore involving external_storage
mission.description:
### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close #7375, close #7850, close #7880, close tidb-challenge-program/bug-hunting-issue#72

Problem Summary:

The backup/restore did not have timeout and retry. This causes a single spurious network error to either catastrophically kill the entire BR task or block the BR thread pool forever.

### What is changed and how it works?

What's Changed:

1. ("Restore" supports retrying externally through pingcap/br#298, so for now we're not going to implement retry for "Restore").
2. The "Backup" gRPC produces a stream, which cannot be retried externally. Therefore, we implemented retry directly in the backup service (`ExternalStorage::write` in particular) to fix #7850.
3. The produced backup file can have size up to 135 MiB. This is not a good idea to retry the upload entirely. So we implemented [Multipart Upload](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/dev/mpuoverview.html) on S3. Unfortunately `tame-gcs` does not support resumable uplo

resolution.summary:
cherry-pick #7917 to release-3.1

---

### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close #7375, close #7850, close #7880, close tidb-challenge-program/bug-hunting-issue#72

Problem Summary:

The backup/restore did not have timeout and retry. This causes a single spurious network error to either catastrophically kill the entire BR task or block the BR thread pool forever.

### What is changed and how it works?

What's Changed:

1. ("Restore" supports retrying externally through pingcap/br#298, so for now we're not going to implement retry for "Restore").
2. The "Backup" gRPC produces a stream, which cannot be retried externally. Therefore, we implemented retry directly in the backup service (`ExternalStorage::write` in particular) to fix #7850.
3. The produced backup file can have size up to 135 MiB. This is not a good idea to retry the upload entirely. So we implemented [Multipart Upload](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/dev/mpuoverview.html) on S3. Unfortunately `tame-gcs` does not support resumable upload EmbarkStudios/tame-gcs#24 so this won't work for GCS. (This also fix #7375.)
    * Unfortunately, we need to copy the SST file into memory and clone several times in order to retry. 
4. We have also replaced `reqwest` by `hyper` so those `convert_request` and `convert_response` are no longer needed.
5. For "Restore", we added a timeout around `ExternalStorage::read`, so that a stuck download won't block the thread pool forever, to fix #7880. 

### Relate
steps: ("Restore" supports retrying externally through pingcap/br#298, so for now we're not going to implement retry for "Restore").
The "Backup" gRPC produces a stream, which cannot be retried externally. Therefore, we implemented retry directly in the backup service (`ExternalStorage::write` in particular) to fix #7850.

The produced backup file can have size up to 135 MiB. This is not a good idea to retry the upload entirely. So we implemented [Multipart Upload](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/dev/mpuoverview.html) on S3. Unfortunately `tame-gcs` does not support resumable upload EmbarkStudios/tame-gcs#24 so this won't work for GCS. (This also fix #7375.)
We have also replaced `reqwest` by `hyper` so those `convert_request` and `convert_response` are no longer needed.
For "Restore", we added a timeout around `ExternalStorage::read`, so that a stuck download won't block the thread pool forever, to fix #7880.
